title: fix make clean error
body:
Fix the following error:

```
➜  buildah git:(master) ✗ make clean                                                                                            
rm -f buildah imgtype build                                                                                                 
rm: cannot remove 'build': Is a directory                                                                                    
Makefile:28: recipe for target 'clean' failed
make: *** [clean] Error 1      
```

Signed-off-by: Zhou Hao <zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com>

comments.body: The build directory was added for the COPR Enablement in #460.  It's not causing an error for me, not sure what's going on for you @q384566678 .  @baude any thoughts?

@TomSweeneyRedHat when `build` exists, `make clean` fails as it cannot delete it.

If we need `build` then we have to change `$(RM)` to `$(RM) -r`

I think we should be removing build on a make clean.
So adding -r would be the correct fix.

>The build directory was added for the COPR Enablement ...

@TomSweeneyRedHat I noticed this pr, but `.copr/Makefile` file is gone, and I'm not clear how to create `build` file.
LGTM and happy green test buttons.  Thanks @q384566678 !

:pushpin: Commit a970fe7 has been approved by `rhatdan`
